page: Amazon Web Services g6e.24xlarge is a graphics-intensive instance powered by the Nitro hypervisor. It features 96 vCPUs and 48 physical cores from an AMD EPYC 7R13 processor operating at 3.4 GHz. The system includes 768.0 GB of DDR4 memory running at 3 GHz, providing 8.0 GB of RAM per core. For acceleration, the instance is equipped with four NVIDIA Ada Lovelace L40S GPUs, delivering a combined 179 GB of VRAM. Local storage consists of 3800 GB of NVMe SSD, and the baseline network bandwidth is rated at 200 Gbps.

Benchmark data indicates top-tier performance in multi-core compute and machine learning workloads. The instance achieves top-tier (top 10%) results in stress-ng multi-core CPU performance and PassMark CPU encryption tests. Large language model (LLM) inference speeds for prompt processing and text generation across small (135M), medium (7B), and large (70B) models consistently rank in the top tier. Single-core performance, measured by stress-ng and Geekbench, falls into the strong and average categories, respectively. Memory bandwidth for large uncached blocks and static web serving throughput register as average.

The hardware profile represents a high-density resource allocation, combining substantial GPU acceleration with a high vCPU count and local NVMe storage. This configuration offers qualitative cost efficiency for workloads that can simultaneously leverage parallel GPU processing and high-throughput local storage, avoiding the overhead of external network storage. However, for workloads limited by single-threaded execution or large-block memory bandwidth, the resource density may introduce underutilization tradeoffs. The instance targets large-scale LLM inference, graphics-intensive rendering, multi-threaded database operations, and intensive parallel computing tasks.
description: Amazon Web Services g6e.24xlarge is a graphics-intensive instance designed for demanding compute and acceleration workloads. Powered by the Nitro hypervisor, it features 96 vCPUs from an AMD EPYC 7R13 processor running at 3.4 GHz, 768.0 GB of DDR4 memory, and 3800 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. The hardware configuration includes four NVIDIA Ada Lovelace L40S GPUs with 179 GB of total VRAM, supported by a 200 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Benchmark results show top-tier performance in multi-core CPU execution, cryptographic operations, and large language model inference across various model sizes. Conversely, single-core processing and large-block memory bandwidth perform in the average to strong ranges. This resource-dense profile is designed for multi-GPU machine learning inference, complex simulations, and data-intensive applications requiring high local storage throughput.
og_description: Amazon Web Services g6e.24xlarge is a graphics-intensive instance featuring 96 vCPUs, 768 GB RAM, four NVIDIA L40S GPUs with 179 GB VRAM, and 3800 GB NVMe SSD storage for heavy compute workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services g6e.24xlarge features 96 vCPUs, four NVIDIA L40S GPUs, 768 GB RAM, and 3800 GB NVMe SSD storage for intensive GPU workloads.
tagline: An accelerated computing platform featuring quad NVIDIA L40S GPUs, 96 AMD EPYC vCPUs, and high-speed local NVMe storage.
bullet_points: Four NVIDIA Ada Lovelace L40S GPUs with 179 GB total VRAM.
96 vCPUs powered by AMD EPYC 7R13 processors at 3.4 GHz.
768 GB of DDR4 system memory and 3800 GB of local NVMe SSD storage.
200 Gbps baseline network bandwidth via the Nitro hypervisor.
Top-tier performance in multi-core CPU benchmarks and LLM inference.
Designed for large language model deployment, graphics rendering, and parallel computing.
